perturbation.original_text: The Parties each undertake to the other that for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, they will not, without prior written consent of the other, directly or indirectly through third parties, make or seek to make contact or communication with those Banks, Financial Institutions, government representatives, clients, investors, traders, associates, legal advisors and financial advisors with whom they have been placed into contact with by the other or to whom the names, addresses and other pertinent information has been released to them by the other.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties each undertake to the other that for a period of five (5) years from the date of this Agreement, they will not, without prior written consent of the other, directly or indirectly through third parties, make or seek to make contact or communication with those Banks, Financial Institutions, government representatives, clients, investors, traders, associates, legal advisors and financial advisors with whom they have been placed into contact with by the other or to whom the names, addresses and other pertinent information has been released to them by the other. Notwithstanding the foregoing, either party may contact said entities if compelled by legal process.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an exception to the non-circumvention clause, allowing contact with listed entities if compelled by legal process. However, Section 3(e) states “Not to enter into direct or indirect negotiations with any other's contacts/clients.” This directly contradicts the new allowance for contact, as 'compelled by legal process' could necessitate negotiations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes irrevocably and unconditionally: a. To ensure that all aspects of each transaction remain confidential. b. Not to disclose, either verbally or in written form, any knowledge that it may obtain at any time in the future, be it either implicit or <PAGE> implied, with respect to the implementation of any proposed transaction. c. Not to circumvent or attempt to circumvent the other. d. Not to disclose to third parties the names, addresses, fax and telephone coordinates of any contact/client revealed by one party to the other. e. Not to enter into direct or indirect negotiations with any other's contacts/clients. f. Not to show, deliver, or cause to be seen, any documents, papers, correspondence, memoranda or copies of such to other than person(s) or entities of any kind, except to those whom are required to maintain confidentiality such as an attorney or tax advisor. g. Each Party shall endeavor to ensure that any of its officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates who, by virtue of their duties may receive the type of information described in this Agreement, are fully obligated to respect the spirit and terms of this Agreements in the same way as each Party. Each Party shall undertakes to have those officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates acknowledge their obligation by Countersigning a copy of this Agreement, thereby binding them to honor the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the non-circumvention clause, allowing contact with entities if compelled by legal process. However, the contradicted text states 'Not to enter into direct or indirect negotiations with any other's contacts/clients.' This directly contradicts the new allowance, as 'compelled by legal process' could necessitate negotiations, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This restriction shall continue to apply after the expiration of this Agreement, and other Agreements entered into between the Parties, without limit in point of time, but shall cease to apply to secrets or information, which came into the public domain through no fault of the Party concerned.
perturbation.changed_text: This restriction shall continue to apply after the expiration of this Agreement, and other Agreements entered into between the Parties, for a period of 1 year, but shall cease to apply to secrets or information, which came into the public domain through no fault of the Party concerned.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the duration of the restriction from 'without limit in point of time' to 'for a period of 1 year' after the expiration of the agreement, it directly contradicts the non-disclosure obligations outlined in section 3(b). This section states 'Not to disclose, either verbally or in written form, any knowledge that it may obtain at any time in the future', implying a perpetual obligation, even after the agreement's initial restrictions expire.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes irrevocably and unconditionally: a. To ensure that all aspects of each transaction remain confidential. b. Not to disclose, either verbally or in written form, any knowledge that it may obtain at any time in the future, be it either implicit or <PAGE> implied, with respect to the implementation of any proposed transaction. c. Not to circumvent or attempt to circumvent the other. d. Not to disclose to third parties the names, addresses, fax and telephone coordinates of any contact/client revealed by one party to the other. e. Not to enter into direct or indirect negotiations with any other's contacts/clients. f. Not to show, deliver, or cause to be seen, any documents, papers, correspondence, memoranda or copies of such to other than person(s) or entities of any kind, except to those whom are required to maintain confidentiality such as an attorney or tax advisor. g. Each Party shall endeavor to ensure that any of its officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates who, by virtue of their duties may receive the type of information described in this Agreement, are fully obligated to respect the spirit and terms of this Agreements in the same way as each Party. Each Party shall undertakes to have those officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates acknowledge their obligation by Countersigning a copy of this Agreement, thereby binding them to honor the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that the restriction would continue to apply 'without limit in point of time.' The changed text modifies this to 'for a period of 1 year.' This directly contradicts the language in the contradicted text, specifically section b, which states 'Not to disclose, either verbally or in written form, any knowledge that it may obtain at any time in the future,' implying a perpetual obligation. The change introduces a temporal limitation where there was none before, creating a clear in-text contradiction as defined by the prompt.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges that any breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement by either party or its employees, agents, representatives or associates may render the seeking of liquidated damages, by the other and the cancellation and termination of all agreements and transactions.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges that any breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement by either party or its employees, agents, representatives or associates may render the seeking of liquidated damages, by the other, however cancellation and termination of all agreements and transactions shall be at the sole discretion of the non-breaching party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 4 originally states that any breach 'may render...cancellation and termination of all agreements and transactions'. The modified version states that cancellation is at the 'sole discretion of the non-breaching party'. This creates a contradiction. The original phrasing suggests automatic right to terminate, while the revised phrasing makes it optional, thereby contradicting the previously stated consequence of a breach.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that any breach of the terms and conditions of this Agreement by either party or its employees, agents, representatives or associates may render the seeking of liquidated damages, by the other, however cancellation and termination of all agreements and transactions shall be at the sole discretion of the non-breaching party.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that a breach 'may render...cancellation and termination'. The changed text says cancellation is at the 'sole discretion of the non-breaching party'. This creates a direct conflict because the original implies an automatic right to terminate upon breach, while the changed version makes it optional. Since the changed text appears in the contradicted text, it creates a structural flaw because it conflicts with the original meaning and causes an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance, or breach of this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by binding arbitration in Las Vegas, Nevada, in accordance with the rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association, The arbitrator(s) may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy.
perturbation.changed_text: Any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance, or breach of this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by non-binding arbitration in Las Vegas, Nevada, in accordance with the rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association, The arbitrator(s) may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'binding arbitration' to 'non-binding arbitration,' it creates a contradiction with the later statement, 'The decision of the arbitrator(s) shall be final, conclusive, and binding on the parties to the arbitration.' Non-binding arbitration would not result in a final and binding decision.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Any dispute or controversy arising out of or relating to any interpretation, construction, performance, or breach of this Agreement shall be resolved exclusively by non-binding arbitration in Las Vegas, Nevada, in accordance with the rules then in effect of the American Arbitration Association, The arbitrator(s) may grant injunctions or other relief in such dispute or controversy. The decision of the arbitrator(s) shall be final, conclusive, and binding on the parties to the arbitration. Judgment may be entered on the arbitrator's decision in any court having jurisdiction. The parties to the arbitration shall each pay an equal share of the costs and expenses of such <PAGE> arbitration, and each of them shall separately pay their counsel fees and expenses.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified 'binding arbitration,' which implies a final and enforceable decision. The changed text replaces this with 'non-binding arbitration,' which is explicitly contradicted by the contradicted text that states, 'The decision of the arbitrator(s) shall be final, conclusive, and binding on the parties to the arbitration.' Non-binding arbitration inherently cannot have a final and binding decision, thus creating a clear contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall endeavor to prevent its officers, employees, agents, representatives, and associates from doing anything, which, if done by Party, would be a breach of this agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party shall endeavor to prevent its officers, employees, agents, representatives, and associates from doing anything, which, if done by Party, would be a breach of this agreement. However, this provision shall not apply to attorneys representing the other party.
perturbation.explanation: Section 1 originally mandates that parties prevent their agents, including representatives, from breaching the agreement. By adding, 'However, this provision shall not apply to attorneys representing the other party', it contradicts section 3(f), which mandates maintaining confidentiality regarding documents unless required to do so by an attorney. The change suggests it is okay for an attorney to do something that the party themselves cannot do, in contradiction to the original intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each party undertakes irrevocably and unconditionally: a. To ensure that all aspects of each transaction remain confidential. b. Not to disclose, either verbally or in written form, any knowledge that it may obtain at any time in the future, be it either implicit or <PAGE> implied, with respect to the implementation of any proposed transaction. c. Not to circumvent or attempt to circumvent the other. d. Not to disclose to third parties the names, addresses, fax and telephone coordinates of any contact/client revealed by one party to the other. e. Not to enter into direct or indirect negotiations with any other's contacts/clients. f. Not to show, deliver, or cause to be seen, any documents, papers, correspondence, memoranda or copies of such to other than person(s) or entities of any kind, except to those whom are required to maintain confidentiality such as an attorney or tax advisor. g. Each Party shall endeavor to ensure that any of its officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates who, by virtue of their duties may receive the type of information described in this Agreement, are fully obligated to respect the spirit and terms of this Agreements in the same way as each Party. Each Party shall undertakes to have those officers, employees, agents, representatives or associates acknowledge their obligation by Countersigning a copy of this Agreement, thereby binding them to honor the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that each party must prevent its agents from breaching the agreement. The changed text adds an exception for attorneys representing the other party. The contradicted text, specifically section f, prohibits disclosing documents to anyone except those required to maintain confidentiality, such as an attorney. The changed text creates a contradiction because it implies an attorney representing the *other* party is exempt from the obligation to prevent breaches, potentially including breaches of confidentiality that the contradicted section aims to prevent. This creates a direct conflict in obligations, where the original section demanded universal prevention of breaches by agents, including attorneys, but the change introduces an exception, leading to an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts each of which shall be binding on each party by whom or on whose behalf it is so executed, but which together shall constitute a single instrument. For the avoidance of doubt, this Agreement shall not be binding on any party hereto unless and until it shall have been executed by or on behalf of all persons expressed to be party hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts each of which shall be binding on each party by whom or on whose behalf it is so executed, but which together shall constitute a single instrument. For the avoidance of doubt, this Agreement shall be binding on any party hereto even if not executed by or on behalf of all persons expressed to be party hereto.
perturbation.explanation: The last sentence of section 8 originally states 'This Agreement shall not be binding on any party hereto unless and until it shall have been executed by or on behalf of all persons expressed to be party hereto.' By changing this to 'This Agreement shall be binding on any party hereto even if not executed by or on behalf of all persons expressed to be party hereto,' it creates an explicit contradiction. One sentence says it requires all signatures to be binding; the other states it is binding even if all signatures aren't present.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts each of which shall be binding on each party by whom or on whose behalf it is so executed, but which together shall constitute a single instrument. For the avoidance of doubt, this Agreement shall be binding on any party hereto even if not executed by or on behalf of all persons expressed to be party hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts itself. The original text says that the agreement is not binding unless all parties sign. The changed text says the agreement is binding even if all parties have not signed. This is a direct and irreconcilable conflict, as one statement negates the other.
